expectations.text: (e1-git-push) Response includes `git push` targeting the feat/payments branch
expectations.evidence: Line 19: `git push -u origin feat/payments` explicitly targets the feat/payments branch.
Line 35: `gh pr create --base main --head feat/payments --title "Add payments feature" --body "Description of changes"` and line 41: `gh pr create` (interactive form).

expectations.text: (e1-pr-title) Response includes a `--title` flag or advises on a PR title format
expectations.evidence: Line 35 includes `--title "Add payments feature"` as part of the gh pr create command.

expectations.text: (e1-pr-body) Response includes a `--body` flag or advises on PR description content
expectations.evidence: Line 35 includes `--body "Description of changes"` as part of the gh pr create command.

eval_feedback: The response fully satisfies all four assertions. It provides a clear `git push -u origin feat/payments` command targeting the correct branch, and a complete `gh pr create` invocation with both `--title` and `--body` flags. The response also covers interactive mode, browser-based alternatives, and worktree-specific caveats, making it thorough and practical.
